subject: [PATCH v2 00/28] vfs/nfsd: add support for CB_NOTIFY callbacks in directory delegations

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the break_lease_block tracepoint displaying misleading information, agreeing to modify it in v2 by showing info about the file_lease (fl) instead of 'new_fl'.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a request to add tracepoints for LEASE_BREAK_* flags in the break_lease function. They have added a new tracepoint, break_lease, and modified the existing trace_break_lease_block() function to include these flags.
reviews.raw_body:
...mostly to show the LEASE_BREAK_* flags.

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>
Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
---
 fs/locks.c                      |  2 ++
 include/trace/events/filelock.h | 33 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 35 insertions(+)

diff --git a/fs/locks.c b/fs/locks.c
index 8b5958f34b61..792c3920b33a 100644
--- a/fs/locks.c
+++ b/fs/locks.c
@@ -1651,6 +1651,8 @@ int __break_lease(struct inode *inode, unsigned int flags)
 	bool want_write = !(flags & LEASE_BREAK_OPEN_RDONLY);
 	int error = 0;
 
+	trace_break_lease(inode, flags);
+
 	type = break_lease_flags_to_type(flags);
 	if (!type)
 		return -EINVAL;
diff --git a/include/trace/events/filelock.h b/include/trace/events/filelock.h
index ef4bb0afb86a..fff0ee2d452d 100644
--- a/include/trace/events/filelock.h
+++ b/include/trace/events/filelock.h
@@ -120,6 +120,39 @@ DEFINE_EVENT(filelock_lock, flock_lock_inode,
 		TP_PROTO(struct inode *inode, struct file_lock *fl, int ret),
 		TP_ARGS(inode, fl, ret));
 
+#define show_lease_break_flags(val)					\
+	__print_flags(val, "|",						\
+		{ LEASE_BREAK_LEASE,		"LEASE" },		\
+		{ LEASE_BREAK_DELEG,		"DELEG" },		\
+		{ LEASE_BREAK_LAYOUT,		"LAYOUT" },		\
+		{ LEASE_BREAK_NONBLOCK,		"NONBLOCK" },		\
+		{ LEASE_BREAK_OPEN_RDONLY,	"OPEN_RDONLY" },	\
+		{ LEASE_BREAK_DIR_CREATE,	"DIR_CREATE" },		\
+		{ LEASE_BREAK_DIR_DELETE,	"DIR_DELETE" },		\
+		{ LEASE_BREAK_DIR_RENAME,	"DIR_RENAME" })
+
+TRACE_EVENT(break_lease,
+	TP_PROTO(struct inode *inode, unsigned int flags),
+
+	TP_ARGS(inode, flags),
+
+	TP_STRUCT__entry(
+		__field(unsigned long, i_ino)
+		__field(dev_t, s_dev)
+		__field(unsigned int, flags)
+	),
+
+	TP_fast_assign(
+		__entry->s_dev = inode->i_sb->s_dev;
+		__entry->i_ino = inode->i_ino;
+		__entry->flags = flags;
+	),
+
+	TP_printk("dev=0x%x:0x%x ino=0x%lx flags=%s",
+		  MAJOR(__entry->s_dev), MINOR(__entry->s_dev),
+		  __entry->i_ino, show_lease_break_flags(__entry->flags))
+);
+
 DECLARE_EVENT_CLASS(filelock_lease,
 	TP_PROTO(struct inode *inode, struct file_lease *fl),
 

-- 
2.53.0

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about how to determine which directory change events should be ignored by nfsd, and responded by adding a new routine (inode_lease_ignore_mask) that returns the union of all ignored inode events for an inode. The routine walks the list of leases, or'ing together their FL_IGN_DIR_* bits. This addresses the issue of how to configure the fsnotify_mark mask based on existing leases.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the server recalling directory delegations when a client requests notifications for directory change events. The author agreed to add support for ignoring lease breaks on directory changes by introducing new flags and modifying existing functions.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a request for additional debugging information to understand how the CB_NOTIFY callbacks are being called. They have added a tracepoint to fsnotify.c and modified the include/trace/events/fsnotify.h file to display the event details in the trace output.
reviews.raw_body:
Add a tracepoint so we can see exactly how this is being called.

Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
---
 fs/notify/fsnotify.c            |  5 ++++
 include/trace/events/fsnotify.h | 51 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 include/trace/misc/fsnotify.h   | 35 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 3 files changed, 91 insertions(+)

diff --git a/fs/notify/fsnotify.c b/fs/notify/fsnotify.c
index 9995de1710e5..5448738635f6 100644
--- a/fs/notify/fsnotify.c
+++ b/fs/notify/fsnotify.c
@@ -14,6 +14,9 @@
 #include <linux/fsnotify_backend.h>
 #include "fsnotify.h"
 
+#define CREATE_TRACE_POINTS
+#include <trace/events/fsnotify.h>
+
 /*
  * Clear all of the marks on an inode when it is being evicted from core
  */
@@ -504,6 +507,8 @@ int fsnotify(__u32 mask, const void *data, int data_type, struct inode *dir,
 	int ret = 0;
 	__u32 test_mask, marks_mask = 0;
 
+	trace_fsnotify(mask, data, data_type, dir, file_name, inode, cookie);
+
 	if (path)
 		mnt = real_mount(path->mnt);
 
diff --git a/include/trace/events/fsnotify.h b/include/trace/events/fsnotify.h
new file mode 100644
index 000000000000..341bbd57a39b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/include/trace/events/fsnotify.h
@@ -0,0 +1,51 @@
+/* SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0 */
+#undef TRACE_SYSTEM
+#define TRACE_SYSTEM fsnotify
+
+#if !defined(_TRACE_FSNOTIFY_H) || defined(TRACE_HEADER_MULTI_READ)
+#define _TRACE_FSNOTIFY_H
+
+#include <linux/tracepoint.h>
+
+#include <trace/misc/fsnotify.h>
+
+TRACE_EVENT(fsnotify,
+	TP_PROTO(__u32 mask, const void *data, int data_type,
+		 struct inode *dir, const struct qstr *file_name,
+		 struct inode *inode, u32 cookie),
+
+	TP_ARGS(mask, data, data_type, dir, file_name, inode, cookie),
+
+	TP_STRUCT__entry(
+		__field(__u32, mask)
+		__field(unsigned long, dir_ino)
+		__field(unsigned long, ino)
+		__field(dev_t, s_dev)
+		__field(int, data_type)
+		__field(u32, cookie)
+		__string(file_name, file_name ? (const char *)file_name->name : "")
+	),
+
+	TP_fast_assign(
+		__entry->mask = mask;
+		__entry->dir_ino = dir ? dir->i_ino : 0;
+		__entry->ino = inode ? inode->i_ino : 0;
+		__entry->s_dev = dir ? dir->i_sb->s_dev :
+				 inode ? inode->i_sb->s_dev : 0;
+		__entry->data_type = data_type;
+		__entry->cookie = cookie;
+		__assign_str(file_name);
+	),
+
+	TP_printk("dev=%d:%d dir=%lu ino=%lu data_type=%d cookie=0x%x mask=0x%x %s name=%s",
+		  MAJOR(__entry->s_dev), MINOR(__entry->s_dev),
+		  __entry->dir_ino, __entry->ino,
+		  __entry->data_type, __entry->cookie,
+		  __entry->mask, show_fsnotify_mask(__entry->mask),
+		  __get_str(file_name))
+);
+
+#endif /* _TRACE_FSNOTIFY_H */
+
+/* This part must be outside protection */
+#include <trace/define_trace.h>
diff --git a/include/trace/misc/fsnotify.h b/include/trace/misc/fsnotify.h
new file mode 100644
index 000000000000..a201e1bd6d8c
--- /dev/null
+++ b/include/trace/misc/fsnotify.h
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+/* SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0 */
+/*
+ * Display helpers for fsnotify events
+ */
+
+#include <linux/fsnotify_backend.h>
+
+#define show_fsnotify_mask(mask) \
+	__print_flags(mask, "|", \
+		{ FS_ACCESS,		"ACCESS" }, \
+		{ FS_MODIFY,		"MODIFY" }, \
+		{ FS_ATTRIB,		"ATTRIB" }, \
+		{ FS_CLOSE_WRITE,	"CLOSE_WRITE" }, \
+		{ FS_CLOSE_NOWRITE,	"CLOSE_NOWRITE" }, \
+		{ FS_OPEN,		"OPEN" }, \
+		{ FS_MOVED_FROM,	"MOVED_FROM" }, \
+		{ FS_MOVED_TO,		"MOVED_TO" }, \
+		{ FS_CREATE,		"CREATE" }, \
+		{ FS_DELETE,		"DELETE" }, \
+		{ FS_DELETE_SELF,	"DELETE_SELF" }, \
+		{ FS_MOVE_SELF,		"MOVE_SELF" }, \
+		{ FS_OPEN_EXEC,		"OPEN_EXEC" }, \
+		{ FS_UNMOUNT,		"UNMOUNT" }, \
+		{ FS_Q_OVERFLOW,	"Q_OVERFLOW" }, \
+		{ FS_ERROR,		"ERROR" }, \
+		{ FS_OPEN_PERM,		"OPEN_PERM" }, \
+		{ FS_ACCESS_PERM,	"ACCESS_PERM" }, \
+		{ FS_OPEN_EXEC_PERM,	"OPEN_EXEC_PERM" }, \
+		{ FS_PRE_ACCESS,	"PRE_ACCESS" }, \
+		{ FS_MNT_ATTACH,	"MNT_ATTACH" }, \
+		{ FS_MNT_DETACH,	"MNT_DETACH" }, \
+		{ FS_EVENT_ON_CHILD,	"EVENT_ON_CHILD" }, \
+		{ FS_RENAME,		"RENAME" }, \
+		{ FS_DN_MULTISHOT,	"DN_MULTISHOT" }, \
+		{ FS_ISDIR,		"ISDIR" })

-- 
2.53.0

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about modifying the mask on an existing mark when new directory delegations are set or unset, and responded by adding a new exported function that allows callers to set and clear bits in the mark's mask and does recalculation if something changed.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the lack of information provided to fsnotify consumers when a rename operation replaces an existing file, by introducing a new FSNOTIFY_EVENT_RENAME data type that carries both the moved dentry and the overwritten target inode. The author updated several functions to handle this new type, including fsnotify_move(), fsnotify_data_inode(), fsnotify_data_dentry(), and fsnotify_data_sb(). This change allows fsnotify consumers like nfsd to atomically observe the overwritten file without needing a separate FS_DELETE event.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the nfs_breaker_owns_lease() function, which was incorrectly checking if the lease breaker owns the lease. The author agreed to add a check for nfsd leases by verifying that fl_lmops is set to nfsd_lease_mng_ops.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the removal of NFS4_VERIFIER_SIZE and NFS4_FHSIZE from the UAPI headers, explaining that these constants are defined by the RFC and should be obtained from more authoritative sources.

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that it's unusual for the NFS protocol to report both the original and overwritten nodes in a single rename event, but accepted the design choice.
reviews.raw_body:
It is strange to me that the NFS protocol needs to report the overwritten
node in the same event of the rename, but oh well, fine by me.

Feel free to add:
Reviewed-by: Amir Goldstein <amir73il@gmail.com>

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged the usefulness of including unnecessary information in the protocol, citing the historical context and limitations of RFC5661.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, it's not very useful, but the protocol requires it. Unfortunately
RFC5661 was written before anyone had made a real implementation of
directory delegations. If we were rewriting it today, we'd probably
make that info optional.

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer Jan Kara approved the patch and added a Reviewed-by tag, indicating that he is satisfied with the changes.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer Jan Kara approved the patch and added a Reviewed-by tag, indicating that he has reviewed it.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

2026-04-16.patch_summary: This patch series adds support for CB_NOTIFY callbacks in directory delegations, allowing clients to register interest in a directory and receive notifications about changes within it without losing its lease. The patches include bugfixes, new tracepoints, and the addition of a new FSNOTIFY_EVENT_RENAME data type to handle cross-directory rename events. The series builds on previous work on directory delegation and has been tested with pynfs, but further client-side implementation is needed for complete testing.
